name: Company or business entity incorporation (DCIP)
notes: Supported by the Black Book licence record's applicability to formal business organisations. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Formal business entities register with the Department of Companies and Intellectual Property, and private companies file annual returns.
appliesWhen: On formation of a formal business entity, and annually thereafter for the annual return.

name: ZIMRA taxpayer registration
notes: Supported by the Black Book record's applicability to individuals, companies, partnerships and cooperatives starting a business. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: New businesses register with ZIMRA through TaRMS within the period stated in ZIMRA's new-trader guidance.

name: VAT registration and returns (ZIMRA)
notes: Applicability and return cadences per the Black Book records; the current registration threshold is deliberately not restated here — confirm with ZIMRA. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Persons trading in taxable supplies who meet the compulsory registration rules register for VAT and file returns for their assigned tax period.
appliesWhen: When the business meets the current compulsory VAT registration rules; returns per assigned tax period thereafter.

name: Employer (PAYE) registration and remittance (ZIMRA)
notes: Supported by the Black Book record's applicability to businesses that become employers. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Businesses that become employers register for PAYE, remit monthly, and file the annual ITF 16 return.
appliesWhen: On becoming an employer; monthly remittance and annual return thereafter.

name: NSSA employer registration and POBS contributions
notes: Supported by the Black Book record's applicability to employers of labour. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Employers of labour register with NSSA and remit Pensions and Other Benefits Scheme contributions monthly.
appliesWhen: On becoming an employer of labour; monthly remittance thereafter.

name: Harare municipal business licence
notes: The Black Book record's applicability expressly lists retail and wholesale shops. Harare is an example: each local authority operates its own licensing regime — confirm with the relevant council. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Retail and wholesale shops and food premises in Harare require the council business licence, renewed each licensing year.
appliesWhen: When trading from premises in the City of Harare; renewal each council licensing year.

name: Harare health registration of premises
notes: Applicability limited to premises types listed on the council's form. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Business premises listed on Harare's Health Registration application form, including food premises, require health registration.
appliesWhen: When operating premises of a type listed on the Harare Health Registration form; review or renewal per council process.

name: Bulawayo shop licence
notes: The Black Book record's applicability expressly covers retail and wholesale activity from fixed premises. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Retail and wholesale activity from a shop, store or other fixed premises in Bulawayo requires the shop licence, renewed annually.
appliesWhen: When trading from fixed premises in the City of Bulawayo; renew before 31 December or before expiry.

name: Bulawayo premises registration certificate
notes: Applicability limited to the premises types listed by the Health Inspectorate. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Food premises and other premises listed by the Bulawayo Health Inspectorate require an annually renewed registration certificate.
appliesWhen: When operating listed premises types in Bulawayo; certificate valid to 31 December of the current year.

name: Liquor licence (Liquor Licensing Board)
notes: The Black Book records the licence and its Board; licence classes and any additional council requirements must be confirmed with the Board. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Businesses selling liquor apply to the national Liquor Licensing Board for a liquor licence and renew per Board instructions.
appliesWhen: Before selling liquor; renewals per current licence expiry and Board instructions.

name: Petroleum retail licence (ZERA)
notes: Supported by the Black Book record's applicability to petroleum retail activities requiring a ZERA licence. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Petroleum retail activities require a ZERA petroleum retail licence.

name: LPG retail licence (ZERA)
notes: Supported by the Black Book record's applicability to LPG retail activities requiring a ZERA licence. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: LPG retail activities require a ZERA LPG retail licence.

name: Medicines premises licence (MCAZ)
notes: The Black Book record covers medicines premises and business types subject to MCAZ licensing; the precise premises classes must be confirmed with MCAZ. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Premises dealing in medicines, including retail pharmacies, are subject to MCAZ premises licensing.
appliesWhen: Before operating medicines premises subject to MCAZ licensing.

name: Business membership bodies relevant to retail
notes: Membership is voluntary; these bodies exist per their Black Book records. No retail-specific mandatory association is asserted. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Retailers may voluntarily join cross-sector business membership bodies such as ZNCC and SMEAZ.

name: Competition and fair-trading oversight
notes: The authority exists per its Black Book record, but its specific application to retail transactions was not evidenced in this revision; declared unverified rather than asserted.
obligation: The Competition and Tariff Commission provides economy-wide competition oversight that can affect retail mergers and trade practices.
appliesWhen: Economy-wide; specific applicability to a given retail transaction is not evidenced here.

name: Presumptive taxes for informal traders
notes: Declared research gap: believed relevant from general industry knowledge but not yet evidenced by a Black Book record. No fee, form, or deadline is asserted. Candidate for a future PB content mission.
description: ZIMRA administers presumptive tax regimes relevant to informal and small-scale traders; no dedicated Black Book record exists yet for presumptive tax categories, rates or payment channels.

name: Fiscalisation of VAT-registered retailers
notes: Declared research gap: believed relevant from general industry knowledge but not yet evidenced by a Black Book record. No fee, form, or deadline is asserted. Candidate for a future PB content mission.
description: ZIMRA requires qualifying VAT operators to use fiscal devices interfaced to its systems; no Black Book record yet documents the current fiscalisation categories, device requirements or deadlines.
